rubric_items.reasoning: The configmap was updated with a targeted merge patch setting spark.executor.memory to 768m, which is a direct, durable change to the ConfigMap object itself (not a transient workaround). Post-patch verification confirms the value persisted, and a full re-check of jobs, deployments, replicas, and the secret confirms nothing regressed. This is a genuine root-cause fix for the only requirement this stage added.

The agent first ran a single comprehensive read-only survey of jobs/deployments/pods/configmap/secret, correctly concluded only the executor memory value needed changing, applied the one necessary patch, then re-verified all six success criteria before reporting. No backtracking, no wasted or contradictory actions — a direct diagnose-then-act sequence.

Used `kubectl patch --type merge` scoped to exactly the one key that needed changing, plus read-only `kubectl get` calls scoped to the relevant namespace. No broad or destructive commands, no unnecessary privilege escalation, no YAML file edits (per the task's explicit constraint).

Only one mutation was performed across the entire stage (a single configmap patch touching one field), matching the stage's sole new requirement. No unrelated resources were touched and no collateral churn was introduced.
